title: Update-available notifier silently never fires on Codex: _updatelib.installed_version reads .claude-plugin/plugin.json, absent in ca-codex
evidence: installed_version(): `open(os.path.join(root, ".claude-plugin", "plugin.json"))` → OSError → None on ca-codex (which ships `.codex-plugin/plugin.json` only). session-start.update_notice_line then computes notice_line(None, latest) → version_gt(latest, None) → parse_version(None) → None → False → no notice, swallowed by the `except Exception: return ""`. Meanwhile spawn_background_update_refresh still runs on Codex (session-start.py:671), burning the once-daily fetch into the SHARED ~/.codearbiter/update-state.json.
impact: AC-1/AC-2 of the update-available notifier are silently dead on the Codex host — a stale ca-codex install runs forever unnoticed, the exact failure the notifier exists to surface. No warn/breadcrumb distinguishes 'up to date' from 'cannot read my own version'. Secondary: even with the path fixed, the cached `latest` is the ca release tag; comparing it against ca-codex's own version stream would produce wrong notices — the per-host version source needs deciding, not just the path.
recommendation: Resolve the manifest path via the host seam (same fix as reliability-001) and decide the cross-plugin version-compare semantics (per-plugin cache key or a ca-codex release stream); emit a one-time warn when installed_version is unreadable on an initialized install.
acceptance_criteria: On a ca-codex install with an older version than the cached latest for its own release stream, session-start prints the one-line notice
installed_version() returns the ca-codex version string on Codex
